channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Scientists find why some cells with doubled DNA avoid death
item.description: Researchers have discovered a surprising mechanism that allows some cells to survive after failing to divide properly, despite duplicating their DNA.

item.title: Tiny blue octopus discovered nearly 6,000 feet under Galápagos waters
item.description: A small blue octopus found at great depth near the Galápagos Islands has been officially identified as a new species.

item.title: Ancient bug preserved in amber had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
item.description: Scientists discovered a bizarre 100-million-year-old bug in Myanmar amber with clawed front legs resembling a crab's pincers.

item.title: Prehistoric fish may explain how animals first walked on land
item.description: A study of a 380-million-year-old Antarctic fish skull reveals insights into the evolution of early land-walking animals.
